steps.title: Identify the axis definitions
steps.content: The problem states that the graph displays boiling point $y$ (in degrees Celsius) versus molecular weight $x$ (in grams per mole). So the horizontal axis represents molecular weight, and the vertical axis represents boiling point.

steps.title: Interpret the point (141.32, 172.26)
steps.content: On this graph, the point $(141.32, 172.26)$ has coordinates $x = 141.32$ and $y = 172.26$. Using the axis definitions: the alkane has a molecular weight of $141.32$ grams per mole and a boiling point of $172.26$ degrees Celsius.

steps.title: Find the matching choice
steps.content:
Choice A reverses the values, assigning molecular weight = 172.26 and boiling point = 141.32 — opposite to what the point represents.

Choices C and D claim this point is a minimum or maximum, but since $x = 141.32$ falls roughly in the middle of the range $[1, 280]$, neither extreme is plausible.

Choice B states: a straight-chain alkane with a molecular weight of 141.32 grams per mole has an estimated boiling point of 172.26 degrees Celsius — exactly matching our interpretation.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
